trigger.reason: Meta released Muse Spark 1.1, a new AI coding model with agentic capabilities for enterprise automation, which is a top-tier lab product release likely to trigger sympathy-fade selling in coding tools, low-code platforms, and developer software stocks.

primary_event.headline: Meta enters the crowded AI coding battle with Muse Spark 1.1
primary_event.summary: Meta announced Muse Spark 1.1, an AI coding model designed to handle large agentic workloads, fix bugs, and assist with large code migrations—capabilities targeting enterprise automation use cases. The release positions Meta as a competitor in the AI coding tools space alongside OpenAI Codex and GitHub Copilot.

secondary_events.headline: Anthropic's new Claude feature is quietly selling you on AI

discovery.trigger_acknowledgment: Analyzed Meta's Muse Spark 1.1 AI coding model release, which flagged low-code/dev tools/enterprise software as at-risk sectors.
discovery.run_summary: Today's tape is actually up across the candidate list — these are UP moves, not sell-offs, meaning the trigger's sympathy-fade thesis largely failed to materialize for the curated basket. Since Screen 1's BUY signal requires a NEGATIVE move_pct paired with unjustified panic, none of today's candidates qualify for OVERDONE classification — the tape is telling us these names are being bid on the AI coding release, not faded. This is itself a pedagogically useful null result.

discovery.no_signals_note: The entire candidate basket moved UP on the Meta Muse Spark 1.1 release — semis, cyber, and edge-AI names all rallied rather than sympathy-selling. The trigger detector fired on the model release, but retail did not execute the expected sympathy-fade pattern. Screen 1's BUY logic strictly requires a negative move; grading 'unjustified panic' against a green tape would be miscategorization. Pedagogical takeaway: not every top-tier lab release produces a tradeable blast radius — sometimes the market yawns (or bids AI-adjacent names on the theme), and the correct action is to record the null and move on. Worth tracking whether this basket's non-reaction to Meta specifically (versus, say, Anthropic or OpenAI) is a repeatable pattern — Meta's coding-tool releases may not carry the same sympathy-fade weight as OpenAI/Anthropic releases in this universe.
